text:
I have enough research to compose the complete technical design document. I'll proceed directly to writing it since this is a single-shot deliverable that doesn't benefit from task tracking overhead.

---

# Marketify — Technical Architecture Document (v0.1)

**Scope:** Backend, data, APIs, workflows. UI/UX is out of scope (handled by sibling agent).
**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + TikTok + Instagram/Meta APIs.
**Author's posture:** Opinionated. Where the PRD is silent, I pick a default and justify it. Override any call in review.

---

## 1. PRD Gap Analysis

Each gap lists: **Q** (question), **Why it matters**, **→ Decision**.

### 1.1 Identity & Auth

| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | "No auth" contradicts needing to fetch private creator metrics from TikTok/IG. Without OAuth we cannot call `user.info.stats` or Instagram Graph insights. | Core value prop (pre-conditions on follower/view counts) depends on real metrics. | **Hybrid:** no email/password app auth, but **require TikTok OAuth and/or Instagram OAuth at onboarding as the "proof of handle"**. OAuth access_token + refresh_token IS the session identity. Username is derived from the verified TikTok/IG handle. Device-scoped anon `session_id` cookie is persisted locally. A creator must link at least one platform to do anything meaningful. (See §3e for full recommendation.) |
| 2 | Username uniqueness + collisions | Two users can claim `@bulutyazgan` on different platforms. | **Primary key = internal UUID.** Display identifier = `{platform}:{handle}`. Uniqueness is `(platform, platform_user_id)` from OAuth, NOT the handle string (handles can change). |
| 3 | Can a single user be both creator and lister? | PRD says "chosen at signup." | **One role per account in MVP.** A person who wants both creates two accounts. Keeps RLS and UX simple. Revisit in v2. |
| 4 | Lister auth — listers don't have TikTok "creator" metrics to verify. | How do we trust a company is a company? | **MVP: self-declared, unverified.** Lister signs up with email + org name + username. We accept this at face value. Payment/KYC is post-MVP. |

### 1.2 Listing / Campaign Lifecycle

| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 5 | Listing states beyond active/inactive (draft, closed, archived, expired)? | Listers need to save drafts; finished campaigns need to stop showing. | States: `draft → active → (paused | closed | expired) → archived`. `paused` = inactive, reversible. `closed` = fulfilled/manually ended, terminal. `expired` = past end_date, terminal. |
| 6 | Do listings have end dates / expiration? | Prevents stale campaigns cluttering feed. | **Yes — optional `end_date`.** If null, runs until manually closed. Edge function `expire_listings` runs daily via `pg_cron`. |
| 7 | Submission cap per campaign (max approved submissions)? | A bounty for "10 videos" behaves differently from an open call. | Add `max_submissions` (nullable = unlimited). When `approved_submissions_count >= max_submissions`, listing auto-transitions to `closed`, pending applications stay visitable but cannot be approved. |
| 8 | Applications cap per creator per listing? | Prevents spam reapplications. | **One active application per (creator, listing) pair.** Creator may reapply after rejection/cancellation only if listing is still active AND they pass eligibility again. Enforced by partial unique index (see schema). |
| 9 | "Requirements or price change cancels pending applications" — what exactly counts? | Ambiguous in PRD. | **Listing versioning.** Any change to price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, or max_submissions bumps `version`. All applications in state `pending` on prior version → auto-`cancelled_listing_edit`. Approved applications and submissions are NOT cancelled (the creator already has a contract). Title/description text edits do NOT bump version. |
| 10 | Can lister edit post-conditions without cancelling? | PRD says only requirements/price. | **No — post-condition changes DO bump version and cancel pending applications.** Rationale: post-conditions define what the creator agrees to deliver. Changing them mid-queue is bait-and-switch. |

### 1.3 Applications & Submissions

| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 11 | Creator withdraws application? | Creators change their mind. | **Yes.** Add `withdrawn` state. Only from `pending`. |
| 12 | What if metrics drop below threshold between apply and submission? | Gamed by applying when metrics are fresh, then ghosting real work. | **Re-check at approve-time.** If below threshold when lister tries to approve, approval API returns 409 `INELIGIBLE_NOW` and lister can decide to override (one-click override allowed, logged). **No re-check at submission-time** — once approved, the creator's eligibility is locked. |
| 13 | Multiple videos per submission — one URL or batched? | A campaign may require a set of videos. | **Submission has 1..N video URLs.** Lister's post-conditions include `min_videos` (default 1). Submission is reviewed as a unit. |
| 14 | Resubmission after rejection? | Creators may need to fix and retry. | Add `revision_requested` state. Flow: `pending → revision_requested → pending` (up to 2 revisions; `revision_count` on submission row). After that, reject is final. |
| 15 | Creator reapplies after rejection/cancellation? | See #8. | After `rejected` or `cancelled_listing_edit`: allowed if listing still active. After `withdrawn`: allowed immediately. After lister hits "block creator" (post-MVP): never. |
| 16 | Disputes (creator says submitted, lister says no) | Hard case; immutable log needed. | Write all status transitions to an append-only `events` table. Expose support email in-app. No in-app arbitration in MVP. |
| 17 | Expiration of pending applications/submissions | Listers can ghost. | `applications`: auto-expire 14 days after creation if still `pending`. `submissions`: auto-expire 7 days after creation if still `pending`. Both via `pg_cron`. Configurable on listing in v2. |

### 1.4 Money

| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 18 | Does MVP actually handle payment? | PRD says "price" and "bounty" — ambiguous. | **No real money in MVP.** `price` is display-only ("advertised bounty"). Payment is off-platform (lister contacts creator post-approval). Add a `payment_status` column to submissions for future. Flag this as v2. |

### 1.5 Notifications

| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 19 | Which events notify? Which channels? | PRD says "notified" with no spec. | **Events:** application approved/rejected/cancelled; submission approved/rejected/revision_requested; listing version changed (for applicants); metrics refresh failed. **Channels MVP:** in-app notification row + Expo Push (via Edge Function). Email out of scope. |
| 20 | Realtime for lister seeing new submission? | UX question. | **Supabase Realtime subscriptions** on `submissions` filtered by `listing.lister_id`. Fallback polling on app resume. |

### 1.6 Metrics / External APIs

| # | Gap | Why it matters | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 21 | Metric refresh rate limits | Creator could DDOS our API budget. | **Server-side throttle:** manual refresh once per 6 hours per (user, platform). Background refresh daily via `pg_cron`. |
| 22 | Handling API failures | Expired tokens, rate limit, account deleted. | `metric_snapshots.status = fresh | stale | refreshing | failed`. Show "metrics outdated" badge if `fetched_at < now() - 7 days`. After 3 failed refreshes, mark link `failed_fetch` and prompt re-OAuth. |
| 23 | Deleted / private / removed video at submission time | Creator submits, then deletes video. | Validate URL via oEmbed at submission time (reject if 404). Re-validate via Edge Function on a nightly sweep for `approved` submissions — flag but don't change state. |
| 24 | Post-submission performance verification | Lister wants to reward high-performing videos (v2 feature). | Out of MVP scope, but store `video_external_id` so we can later call `GET /v2/video/query/` (TikTok) or `GET /<IG_MEDIA_ID>?fields=insights` (IG) to fetch view/like counts. |

### 1.7 Security / Abuse

| # | Gap | Why | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| 25 | Lister posts campaign that's spam/illegal | Marketplace trust. | Manual admin review post-MVP. MVP: all listings live immediately; add `report_listing` button → admin table. |
| 26 | Creator submits same video to 10 listings | Double-dipping. | Enforce `UNIQUE(submission.video_url)` globally? No — same video might legitimately match two campaigns. Instead: flag and surface to lister. No hard block MVP. |

---

## 2. State Machines

Notation: `transition [actor: user|lister|system|time]`.

### 2.1 Listing

```
(init) ──create[lister]──> draft
draft ──publish[lister]──> active
active ──pause[lister]──> paused
paused ──resume[lister]──> active
active ──close[lister]──> closed
active ──end_date_passed[time]──> expired
active ──max_submissions_reached[system]──> closed
closed ──archive[lister]──> archived
expired ──archive[lister|time:30d]──> archived
paused ──archive[lister]──> archived

Side-effect on any edit that mutates price/pre-cond/post-cond/samples/max_submissions while active:
  version++ AND trigger cascade (see §2.3)
```

Terminal states: `archived`.

### 2.2 Application

```
(init) ──submit[creator, gated by eligibility]──> pending
pending ──approve[lister]──> approved
pending ──reject[lister]──> rejected
pending ──withdraw[creator]──> withdrawn
pending ──listing_version_bump[system]──> cancelled_listing_edit
pending ──14d_no_action[time]──> expired
pending ──listing_closed_or_archived[system]──> cancelled_listing_closed

approved ──submission_created[system]──> (stays approved; submission owns downstream state)
approved ──listing_archived[system]──> cancelled_listing_closed  // rare edge case
```

Terminal: `approved` (once submission exists), `rejected`, `withdrawn`, `cancelled_*`, `expired`.

### 2.3 Submission

```
(init, requires application.status=approved) ──create[creator]──> pending
pending ──approve[lister]──> approved
pending ──reject[lister]──> rejected
pending ──request_revision[lister]──> revision_requested
revision_requested ──resubmit[creator]──> pending   // max 2 revisions
pending ──7d_no_action[time]──> expired
```

Terminal: `approved`, `rejected`, `expired`.

### 2.4 Social Link

```
(init) ──oauth_complete[creator]──> linked
linked ──refresh_success[system|creator]──> linked
linked ──refresh_fail x3[system]──> failed_fetch
failed_fetch ──reauth[creator]──> linked
linked ──unlink[creator]──> unlinked
```

### 2.5 Metric Snapshot

```
(init, on link) ──fetch_queued[system]──> refreshing
refreshing ──success[system]──> fresh
refreshing ──fail[system]──> failed
fresh ──age > 24h[time]──> stale
stale ──fetch_queued[creator_manual | system_daily]──> refreshing
failed ──retry[system|creator]──> refreshing
```

Rule: eligibility filtering uses `fresh` OR `stale` (not `failed`). Display warning if `stale`.

---

## 3. Best-Practice Research (context7-verified)

### 3a. TikTok — Creator Metrics

**Source:** `/websites/developers_tiktok_doc` — `https://developers.tiktok.com/doc/tiktok-api-v2-get-user-info`, `https://developers.tiktok.com/doc/tiktok-api-scopes`.

- **Endpoint:** `GET https://open.tiktokapis.com/v2/user/info/` with `fields=follower_count,following_count,likes_count,video_count,username,is_verified,display_name`.
- **Required scopes:**
  - `user.info.basic` (open_id, display_name, avatar) — auto-granted with Login Kit.
  - `user.info.profile` (bio, verified, deep link) — optional.
  - `user.info.stats` — **REQUIRED for follower_count, likes_count, video_count**.
  - `video.list` — required to page `POST /v2/video/list/` and derive avg views from last N videos.
- **Auth flow:** OAuth 2.0 via Login Kit. We get `access_token` (short-lived) + `refresh_token` (typically 365 days). Must refresh before expiry.
- **Video stats per video:** `view_count`, `like_count`, `comment_count`, `share_count` available on Video Object (`/v2/video/list/` with `fields=id,view_count,like_count,...`). This is how we compute `avg_views_last_n`.
- **Rate limits:** TikTok documents per-app QPS on each endpoint (varies; typically 10 QPS per app for `/v2/user/info/`). We MUST not fan-out concurrent refreshes; use a queue.
- **Research API** (`/research/user/info/`) surfaces `follower_count` publicly but is gated behind a researcher approval process — **not available to us in MVP**.
- **oEmbed** (`https://www.tiktok.com/oembed?url=...`) is public and unauthenticated but returns ONLY embed HTML + thumbnail + author — **no follower count, no view count**. Useful for rendering submitted videos, not for eligibility.

**Conclusion:** No path to real follower/view counts without TikTok OAuth on the creator side. "No auth, just handles" from PRD is infeasible for pre-conditions.

### 3b. Instagram — Creator Metrics

**Source:** `/websites/developers_facebook_instagram-platform` — `https://developers.facebook.com/docs/instagram-platform/instagram-graph-api/reference/ig-user/business_discovery`, `https://developers.facebook.com/docs/instagram-platform/insights`.

- **`business_discovery`** is the only Graph API path that returns follower_count/media_count for *another* account by username. Important limitations:
  - The *querying* account must be an Instagram Business or Creator account.
  - The *target* account must also be Business or Creator (personal accounts are invisible to this API).
  - Requires permissions `instagram_basic`, `instagram_manage_insights`, `pages_read_engagement` on the querying user's access token.
  - Public-looking but NOT actually unauthenticated.
- **Direct account (own metrics):** `GET /<IG_USER_ID>?fields=followers_count,media_count,username,biography` — Business/Creator accounts only.
- **Media insights:** `GET /<IG_MEDIA_ID>?fields=insights.metric(views,likes,comments,shares,...)`. Note per-changelog: `impressions`, `plays`, and `clips_replays_count` deprecated April 2025; use `views` going forward.
- **Instagram Basic Display API:** deprecated by Meta for new apps; do not use.
- **oEmbed** (`/instagram_oembed`) — public thumbnail + author only; requires app access token; no metrics.

**Conclusion:** For IG we need the creator to log in via Instagram OAuth **AND** they must have a Business/Creator account (switch is free and in-app on IG). Personal IG accounts = we cannot fetch metrics, period.

### 3c. Displaying a Submitted TikTok Video

- Use oEmbed (`GET https://www.tiktok.com/oembed?url=<share_url>`) → returns `html`, `thumbnail_url`, `author_name`, `title`. Renderable via WebView.
- Alternative: construct `embed_link = https://www.tiktok.com/embed/v2/<video_id>` and load in WebView directly.
- **Performance verification:** requires the creator's OAuth token AND `video.list` scope, then `POST /v2/video/list/` to find the video and read `view_count`. Out of MVP scope.

### 3d. Displaying a Submitted Instagram Reel

- `GET /instagram_oembed?url=<reel_url>&access_token=<app_token>` → thumbnail + attribution. No embed HTML as full-featured as TikTok.
- Recommended: parse the reel shortcode from URL (`/reel/{shortcode}/` or `/p/{shortcode}/`), display thumbnail from oEmbed, and deep-link to Instagram app on tap.
- **Performance verification:** the creator must have linked their IG Business account AND we need the `IG_MEDIA_ID`. Resolve via `ig_hashtag_search` / the creator's own `/me/media` — out of MVP scope.

### 3e. MVP Feasibility with "No Auth, No Verification"

**I am overriding the PRD on this point.** The constraint as written is incompatible with the core value proposition.

**Recommended MVP path:**

1. **No app-level auth (email/password).** Correct, keep this.
2. **REQUIRE TikTok OAuth and/or Instagram OAuth at creator onboarding.** At least one. Treat the OAuth account as the identity. No handle is typed by hand.
3. **Self-reported handles rejected entirely.** Metrics come from OAuth calls only.
4. **Listers sign up with username + email** (no social OAuth required — they aren't being filtered on metrics).
5. For IG specifically, require Business/Creator conversion in onboarding flow (show a 3-step guide). If creator only has TikTok, they see only TikTok-gated listings.

Rationale: a marketplace built on unverified follower counts is a fraud vector on day one. Listers pay (or will pay) for access to creators with real audiences. Fake metrics = churn. OAuth is free and a one-time friction.

**Scope cut alternative** (if the above is rejected): launch as "discovery only" — no pre-conditions, creators browse all listings, apply manually, lister eyeballs the handle. Don't ship pre-conditions at all in v1. (See §8.)

---

## 4. Supabase Database Schema

Applied rules from `supabase-postgres-best-practices`: RLS enabled on all public tables; FKs indexed; partial indexes for hot filters; `security_invoker` on views; `security definer` functions in a private schema; composite indexes ordered by selectivity.

### 4.1 Tables Overview

| Table | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `users` | Unified account row; role-gated. |
| `creator_profiles` | 1:1 extension for creator-specific fields. |
| `lister_profiles` | 1:1 extension for lister-specific fields. |
| `social_links` | OAuth-linked TikTok/IG accounts per user. |
| `metric_snapshots` | Historized metric data per social_link. Latest-flagged. |
| `listings` | Core campaign row. Mutable fields live here. |
| `listing_versions` | Immutable snapshots of versioned fields. Applications FK to a version. |
| `listing_conditions` | Pre + post conditions, structured rows tied to a listing_version. |
| `sample_videos` | Sample video URLs per listing_version. |
| `applications` | Creator → listing_version. |
| `submissions` | Final deliverables linked to an approved application. |
| `submission_videos` | N video URLs per submission. |
| `notifications` | In-app notification inbox. |
| `push_tokens` | Expo push tokens per user. |
| `events` | Append-only audit log. |
| `oauth_states` | CSRF/state tokens for OAuth callbacks (short-lived). |

### 4.2 Enums

```sql
create type user_role as enum ('creator', 'lister');
create type platform as enum ('tiktok', 'instagram');
create type listing_status as enum ('draft', 'active', 'paused', 'closed', 'expired', 'archived');
create type application_status as enum (
  'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'withdrawn',
  'cancelled_listing_edit', 'cancelled_listing_closed', 'expired'
);
create type submission_status as enum (
  'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'revision_requested', 'expired'
);
create type social_link_status as enum ('linked', 'unlinked', 'failed_fetch');
create type metric_status as enum ('fresh', 'stale', 'refreshing', 'failed');
create type condition_kind as enum ('pre', 'post');
create type condition_metric as enum (
  'min_followers', 'min_avg_views_last_n', 'min_total_likes',
  'min_videos_posted', 'verified_only',
  'post_min_video_duration_sec', 'post_max_video_duration_sec',
  'post_min_video_count', 'post_must_mention', 'post_family_friendly',
  'post_must_tag_account'
);
create type notification_kind as enum (
  'application_approved', 'application_rejected', 'application_cancelled',
  'submission_received', 'submission_approved', 'submission_rejected',
  'submission_revision_requested', 'listing_version_changed',
  'metrics_refresh_failed'
);
```

### 4.3 Denormalization Notes

- `creator_profiles.latest_follower_count_tiktok`, `latest_follower_count_instagram`, `latest_avg_views_tiktok`, `latest_avg_views_instagram`, `metrics_fetched_at` — denormalized from `metric_snapshots` for **O(1) eligibility filtering** across thousands of listings. Kept in sync by trigger.
- `listings.current_version_id` — FK to `listing_versions`, denormalized to avoid an extra join on every feed query.
- `listings.active_pending_applications_count`, `approved_submissions_count` — counters, trigger-maintained.
- `applications.listing_version_id` — denormalized so cancellation logic becomes a single UPDATE when version changes.

### 4.4 RLS Model (without Supabase Auth)

Since we have no `auth.uid()`, we'll use a custom JWT issued by our `auth_signin` edge function (signed with project JWT secret, same verification path Supabase uses). JWT payload includes:
```json
{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "creator|lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
```
RLS policies read `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'` as the user id. **`sub` is stored in `app_metadata`-equivalent claims we control (signed by us), not user-editable — safe for authz decisions.** We explicitly do NOT use `user_metadata` for authorization (per Supabase security checklist).

### 4.5 Indexes — Key Decisions

- Feed query: `listings WHERE status='active' ORDER BY created_at DESC` — partial index on `(status, created_at DESC) WHERE status='active'`.
- Eligibility pre-filter: denormalized creator metrics compared client-side after server returns filterable listings; server supplies `min_required_followers` column per listing row (computed — see §6) indexed as `btree`.
- Application uniqueness: `UNIQUE (listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status='pending' OR status='approved'` — partial unique.
- Notifications inbox: `(user_id, read_at NULLS FIRST, created_at DESC)`.

### 4.6 Triggers / Functions

All in private schema `app_private` (security-definer path).

| Function | Trigger |
|---|---|
| `app_private.handle_listing_mutation()` | BEFORE UPDATE on `listings` — if any versioned field changed, INSERT into `listing_versions`, set `current_version_id`, and queue application cascade. |
| `app_private.cascade_cancel_pending_apps(listing_id, reason)` | Called by trigger + edge functions. Updates applications + emits notifications. |
| `app_private.denorm_creator_metrics()` | AFTER INSERT on `metric_snapshots` where `is_latest=true` — pushes to `creator_profiles`. |
| `app_private.expire_stale()` | Called by `pg_cron` daily. Expires pending applications (14d) + pending submissions (7d) + past-end_date listings. |
| `app_private.auto_close_on_max_submissions()` | AFTER UPDATE on `submissions` when status → `approved` — if count >= max_submissions, close listing. |
| `app_private.emit_event()` | Helper called by every state-changing function; inserts into `events`. |

### 4.7 Full Migration SQL

```sql
-- =========================================================
-- 0. Extensions & schemas
-- =========================================================
create extension if not exists "pgcrypto";
create extension if not exists "pg_cron";

create schema if not exists app_private;
revoke all on schema app_private from anon, authenticated;

-- =========================================================
-- 1. Enums
-- =========================================================
create type user_role as enum ('creator', 'lister');
create type platform as enum ('tiktok', 'instagram');
create type listing_status as enum ('draft', 'active', 'paused', 'closed', 'expired', 'archived');
create type application_status as enum (
  'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'withdrawn',
  'cancelled_listing_edit', 'cancelled_listing_closed', 'expired'
);
create type submission_status as enum (
  'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'revision_requested', 'expired'
);
create type social_link_status as enum ('linked', 'unlinked', 'failed_fetch');
create type metric_status as enum ('fresh', 'stale', 'refreshing', 'failed');
create type condition_kind as enum ('pre', 'post');
create type condition_metric as enum (
  'min_followers', 'min_avg_views_last_n', 'min_total_likes',
  'min_videos_posted', 'verified_only',
  'post_min_video_duration_sec', 'post_max_video_duration_sec',
  'post_min_video_count', 'post_must_mention', 'post_family_friendly',
  'post_must_tag_account'
);
create type notification_kind as enum (
  'application_approved', 'application_rejected', 'application_cancelled',
  'submission_received', 'submission_approved', 'submission_rejected',
  'submission_revision_requested', 'listing_version_changed',
  'metrics_refresh_failed'
);

-- =========================================================
-- 2. users
-- =========================================================
create table public.users (
  id             uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  role           user_role not null,
  username       citext not null unique,   -- display handle, unique case-insensitively
  email          citext unique,            -- nullable for creators (OAuth-only)
  created_at     timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at     timestamptz not null default now(),
  deleted_at     timestamptz
);
create index on public.users (role) where deleted_at is null;

-- =========================================================
-- 3. creator_profiles (denormalized for fast filtering)
-- =========================================================
create table public.creator_profiles (
  user_id                            uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  display_name                       text,
  bio                                text,
  country                            text,
  -- denormalized latest metrics (nullable until first fetch)
  tiktok_follower_count              integer,
  tiktok_avg_views_last_10           integer,
  tiktok_total_likes                 bigint,
  tiktok_video_count                 integer,
  tiktok_is_verified                 boolean,
  instagram_follower_count           integer,
  instagram_avg_views_last_10        integer,
  instagram_media_count              integer,
  metrics_fetched_at                 timestamptz,
  created_at                         timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at                         timestamptz not null default now()
);
-- indexes so eligibility pre-filter on lister side is cheap
create index on public.creator_profiles (tiktok_follower_count);
create index on public.creator_profiles (instagram_follower_count);

-- =========================================================
-- 4. lister_profiles
-- =========================================================
create table public.lister_profiles (
  user_id      uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  org_name     text not null,
  website_url  text,
  logo_path    text,          -- Supabase Storage path
  description  text,
  created_at   timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at   timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- =========================================================
-- 5. social_links
-- =========================================================
create table public.social_links (
  id                    uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id               uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  platform              platform not null,
  platform_user_id      text not null,        -- TikTok open_id / IG user id
  handle                text not null,        -- denormalized handle at link time
  status                social_link_status not null default 'linked',
  access_token_enc      text,                 -- encrypted via pgsodium in v2; TEXT for MVP
  refresh_token_enc     text,
  token_expires_at      timestamptz,
  scopes                text[],
  last_refresh_at       timestamptz,
  fail_count            smallint not null default 0,
  created_at            timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at            timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (platform, platform_user_id)
);
create unique index social_links_user_platform_uniq
  on public.social_links (user_id, platform) where status <> 'unlinked';

-- =========================================================
-- 6. metric_snapshots (historized)
-- =========================================================
create table public.metric_snapshots (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  social_link_id    uuid not null references public.social_links(id) on delete cascade,
  status            metric_status not null,
  follower_count    integer,
  following_count   integer,
  total_likes       bigint,
  video_count       integer,
  avg_views_last_10 integer,
  is_verified       boolean,
  raw_payload       jsonb,                    -- raw API response for forensics
  fetched_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  is_latest         boolean not null default true,
  error_message     text
);
create index on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, fetched_at desc);
create unique index metric_snapshots_latest
  on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id) where is_latest;

-- =========================================================
-- 7. listings + versioning
-- =========================================================
create table public.listings (
  id                               uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  lister_id                        uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete restrict,
  status                           listing_status not null default 'draft',
  title                            text not null,
  description                      text,
  -- versioned fields live ALSO on listing_versions for historical integrity
  price_cents                      integer not null check (price_cents >= 0),
  currency                         text not null default 'USD',
  max_submissions                  integer,                 -- nullable = unlimited
  approved_submissions_count       integer not null default 0,
  active_pending_applications_count integer not null default 0,
  end_date                         timestamptz,
  current_version_id               uuid,                    -- FK set after row exists
  version_number                   integer not null default 1,
  created_at                       timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at                       timestamptz not null default now(),
  published_at                     timestamptz,
  closed_at                        timestamptz
);
create index listings_feed_idx
  on public.listings (status, created_at desc) where status = 'active';
create index listings_lister_idx on public.listings (lister_id, status);

create table public.listing_versions (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  listing_id        uuid not null references public.listings(id) on delete cascade,
  version_number    integer not null,
  price_cents       integer not null,
  currency          text not null,
  max_submissions   integer,
  snapshot          jsonb not null,         -- full snapshot for audit
  created_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (listing_id, version_number)
);
alter table public.listings
  add constraint listings_current_version_fk
  foreign key (current_version_id) references public.listing_versions(id);

-- =========================================================
-- 8. listing_conditions (pre + post, data-driven)
-- =========================================================
create table public.listing_conditions (
  id                   uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  listing_version_id   uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
  kind                 condition_kind not null,
  metric               condition_metric not null,
  platform             platform,                 -- null if not platform-specific
  operator             text not null default 'gte' check (operator in ('gte','lte','eq','contains','bool')),
  numeric_threshold    numeric,
  text_threshold       text,
  bool_threshold       boolean,
  created_at           timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index on public.listing_conditions (listing_version_id, kind);

-- =========================================================
-- 9. sample_videos
-- =========================================================
create table public.sample_videos (
  id                   uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  listing_version_id   uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
  platform             platform not null,
  url                  text not null,
  caption              text,
  sort_order           smallint not null default 0
);
create index on public.sample_videos (listing_version_id);

-- =========================================================
-- 10. applications
-- =========================================================
create table public.applications (
  id                   uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  listing_id           uuid not null references public.listings(id) on delete restrict,
  listing_version_id   uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete restrict,
  creator_id           uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete restrict,
  status               application_status not null default 'pending',
  cover_note           text,
  created_at           timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at           timestamptz not null default now(),
  decided_at           timestamptz,
  decision_note        text
);
create index on public.applications (listing_id, status);
create index on public.applications (creator_id, status, created_at desc);
create unique index applications_open_uniq
  on public.applications (listing_id, creator_id)
  where status in ('pending','approved');

-- =========================================================
-- 11. submissions
-- =========================================================
create table public.submissions (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  application_id    uuid not null references public.applications(id) on delete restrict,
  status            submission_status not null default 'pending',
  cover_note        text,
  revision_count    smallint not null default 0,
  created_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  decided_at        timestamptz,
  decision_note     text
);
create unique index submissions_open_uniq
  on public.submissions (application_id)
  where status in ('pending','revision_requested','approved');
create index on public.submissions (status, created_at desc);

create table public.submission_videos (
  id              uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  submission_id   uuid not null references public.submissions(id) on delete cascade,
  platform        platform not null,
  url             text not null,
  external_id     text,           -- parsed from URL
  oembed_cached   jsonb,
  last_validated_at timestamptz,
  sort_order      smallint not null default 0
);
create index on public.submission_videos (submission_id);

-- =========================================================
-- 12. notifications + push_tokens
-- =========================================================
create table public.notifications (
  id          uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id     uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  kind        notification_kind not null,
  payload     jsonb not null,
  read_at     timestamptz,
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index notifications_inbox_idx
  on public.notifications (user_id, created_at desc);
create index notifications_unread_idx
  on public.notifications (user_id) where read_at is null;

create table public.push_tokens (
  id          uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id     uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  expo_token  text not null unique,
  platform    text not null check (platform in ('ios','android')),
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
  last_seen_at timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- =========================================================
-- 13. events (append-only audit log)
-- =========================================================
create table public.events (
  id          bigserial primary key,
  actor_id    uuid,
  entity      text not null,    -- 'listing'|'application'|'submission'|...
  entity_id   uuid not null,
  action      text not null,
  old_state   jsonb,
  new_state   jsonb,
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index on public.events (entity, entity_id, created_at desc);

-- =========================================================
-- 14. oauth_states (short-lived CSRF tokens)
-- =========================================================
create table public.oauth_states (
  state       text primary key,
  platform    platform not null,
  user_id     uuid references public.users(id) on delete cascade, -- nullable for signup
  code_verifier text,
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
  expires_at  timestamptz not null default (now() + interval '10 minutes')
);
create index on public.oauth_states (expires_at);

-- =========================================================
-- 15. RLS — enable on all public tables
-- =========================================================
alter table public.users enable row level security;
alter table public.creator_profiles enable row level security;
alter table public.lister_profiles enable row level security;
alter table public.social_links enable row level security;
alter table public.metric_snapshots enable row level security;
alter table public.listings enable row level security;
alter table public.listing_versions enable row level security;
alter table public.listing_conditions enable row level security;
alter table public.sample_videos enable row level security;
alter table public.applications enable row level security;
alter table public.submissions enable row level security;
alter table public.submission_videos enable row level security;
alter table public.notifications enable row level security;
alter table public.push_tokens enable row level security;
alter table public.events enable row level security;
alter table public.oauth_states enable row level security;

-- Helper: returns current user id from custom JWT sub claim
create or replace function public.current_user_id() returns uuid
language sql stable as $$
  select nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid
$$;

create or replace function public.current_user_role() returns user_role
language sql stable as $$
  select (auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role
$$;

-- users: self-read, self-update limited
create policy users_self_read on public.users
  for select using (id = public.current_user_id());
create policy users_self_update on public.users
  for update using (id = public.current_user_id())
  with check (id = public.current_user_id());

-- creator_profiles: owner full, public read of display fields via view
create policy cp_self_all on public.creator_profiles
  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
-- listers need to see creator profiles of their applicants — via a security_invoker view
create view public.v_creator_public with (security_invoker = true) as
  select user_id, display_name, country,
         tiktok_follower_count, instagram_follower_count,
         tiktok_avg_views_last_10, instagram_avg_views_last_10,
         metrics_fetched_at
  from public.creator_profiles;

create policy cp_lister_read_applicants on public.creator_profiles
  for select using (
    public.current_user_role() = 'lister'
    and exists (
      select 1 from public.applications a
      join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
      where a.creator_id = creator_profiles.user_id
        and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()
    )
  );

-- listings: anyone authenticated reads active/paused/closed; owner reads all; owner writes
create policy listings_public_read on public.listings
  for select using (status in ('active','paused','closed','expired'));
create policy listings_owner_all on public.listings
  for all using (lister_id = public.current_user_id())
  with check (lister_id = public.current_user_id());

create policy lv_public_read on public.listing_versions
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.listings l
            where l.id = listing_versions.listing_id
              and (l.status in ('active','paused','closed','expired')
                   or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()))
  );

create policy lc_public_read on public.listing_conditions
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.listing_versions lv
            join public.listings l on l.id = lv.listing_id
            where lv.id = listing_conditions.listing_version_id
              and (l.status in ('active','paused','closed','expired')
                   or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()))
  );

create policy sv_public_read on public.sample_videos
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.listing_versions lv
            join public.listings l on l.id = lv.listing_id
            where lv.id = sample_videos.listing_version_id
              and (l.status in ('active','paused','closed','expired')
                   or l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()))
  );

-- applications: creator sees own; lister sees their listing's
create policy apps_creator_read on public.applications
  for select using (creator_id = public.current_user_id());
create policy apps_lister_read on public.applications
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.listings l
            where l.id = applications.listing_id
              and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())
  );
-- writes go through RPCs (no direct insert/update from client)

-- submissions: creator of the parent app + lister of parent listing
create policy subs_creator_read on public.submissions
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.applications a
            where a.id = submissions.application_id
              and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id())
  );
create policy subs_lister_read on public.submissions
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.applications a
            join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
            where a.id = submissions.application_id
              and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())
  );

create policy sv_rw on public.submission_videos
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.submissions s
            where s.id = submission_videos.submission_id
            and (exists (select 1 from public.applications a
                         where a.id = s.application_id and a.creator_id = public.current_user_id())
                 or exists (select 1 from public.applications a
                            join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
                            where a.id = s.application_id and l.lister_id = public.current_user_id())))
  );

-- social_links + metric_snapshots: owner only
create policy sl_owner on public.social_links
  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
create policy ms_owner on public.metric_snapshots
  for select using (
    exists (select 1 from public.social_links sl
            where sl.id = metric_snapshots.social_link_id
              and sl.user_id = public.current_user_id())
  );

-- notifications: owner
create policy notif_owner on public.notifications
  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());
create policy push_owner on public.push_tokens
  for all using (user_id = public.current_user_id())
  with check (user_id = public.current_user_id());

-- events: no direct reads from clients (server/admin only via service_role)
create policy events_none on public.events for select using (false);
-- oauth_states: none from clients
create policy os_none on public.oauth_states for all using (false) with check (false);

-- =========================================================
-- 16. Triggers / functions
-- =========================================================
create or replace function app_private.bump_listing_version()
returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
declare
  versioned_changed boolean;
  new_version_id uuid;
begin
  versioned_changed := (
    old.price_cents is distinct from new.price_cents
    or old.currency is distinct from new.currency
    or old.max_submissions is distinct from new.max_submissions
  );
  if versioned_changed and new.status = 'active' then
    new.version_number := old.version_number + 1;
    insert into public.listing_versions(listing_id, version_number, price_cents, currency, max_submissions, snapshot)
      values (new.id, new.version_number, new.price_cents, new.currency, new.max_submissions,
              to_jsonb(new))
      returning id into new_version_id;
    new.current_version_id := new_version_id;

    update public.applications
      set status = 'cancelled_listing_edit', updated_at = now()
      where listing_id = new.id and status = 'pending';

    insert into public.notifications(user_id, kind, payload)
      select creator_id, 'listing_version_changed',
             jsonb_build_object('listing_id', new.id, 'new_version', new.version_number)
      from public.applications
      where listing_id = new.id and status = 'cancelled_listing_edit';
  end if;
  return new;
end $$;

create trigger trg_bump_listing_version
  before update on public.listings
  for each row execute function app_private.bump_listing_version();

create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
declare
  sl public.social_links%rowtype;
begin
  if new.is_latest then
    -- mark older snapshots not latest
    update public.metric_snapshots
      set is_latest = false
      where social_link_id = new.social_link_id and id <> new.id and is_latest;

    select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
    if sl.platform = 'tiktok' then
      update public.creator_profiles
        set tiktok_follower_count = new.follower_count,
            tiktok_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
            tiktok_total_likes = new.total_likes,
            tiktok_video_count = new.video_count,
            tiktok_is_verified = new.is_verified,
            metrics_fetched_at = new.fetched_at,
            updated_at = now()
      where user_id = sl.user_id;
    elsif sl.platform = 'instagram' then
      update public.creator_profiles
        set instagram_follower_count = new.follower_count,
            instagram_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
            instagram_media_count = new.video_count,
            metrics_fetched_at = new.fetched_at,
            updated_at = now()
      where user_id = sl.user_id;
    end if;
  end if;
  return new;
end $$;

create trigger trg_denorm_metrics
  after insert on public.metric_snapshots
  for each row execute function app_private.denorm_metrics();

create or replace function app_private.submission_side_effects()
returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
declare
  lst public.listings%rowtype;
begin
  if tg_op = 'UPDATE' and new.status = 'approved' and old.status <> 'approved' then
    select l.* into lst
      from public.listings l
      join public.applications a on a.listing_id = l.id
      where a.id = new.application_id;
    update public.listings
      set approved_submissions_count = approved_submissions_count + 1
      where id = lst.id;
    if lst.max_submissions is not null
       and lst.approved_submissions_count + 1 >= lst.max_submissions then
      update public.listings
        set status = 'closed', closed_at = now()
        where id = lst.id;
    end if;
  end if;
  return new;
end $$;

create trigger trg_submission_side_effects
  after update on public.submissions
  for each row execute function app_private.submission_side_effects();

-- Daily cron jobs (schedules configurable)
select cron.schedule('expire-stale', '0 * * * *', $$
  update public.applications
    set status = 'expired', updated_at = now()
    where status = 'pending' and created_at < now() - interval '14 days';
  update public.submissions
    set status = 'expired', updated_at = now()
    where status = 'pending' and created_at < now() - interval '7 days';
  update public.listings
    set status = 'expired'
    where status = 'active' and end_date is not null and end_date < now();
  update public.metric_snapshots
    set status = 'stale'
    where status = 'fresh' and fetched_at < now() - interval '24 hours' and is_latest;
$$);
```

---

## 5. API / Edge Function Spec Sheet

**Transport:**
- **Direct table reads** via `postgrest` (Supabase Data API) for anything RLS-safe (feed browse, notifications inbox).
- **Edge Functions** for anything that (a) calls an external API, (b) performs multi-table writes atomically, (c) enforces server-side business logic, or (d) issues JWTs.
- **Realtime channels** on `applications`, `submissions`, `notifications`.

Roles below refer to custom-JWT `role` claim.

### 5.1 Auth & Identity

#### `POST /auth/signup-creator`
Create creator account, redirect to TikTok or IG OAuth.
```ts
interface Req { role: 'creator'; initial_platform: 'tiktok' | 'instagram'; username?: string; }
interface Res { auth_url: string; state: string; } // 302-style payload
```
Side effects: inserts row into `oauth_states`. No user row yet.
Errors: `400 INVALID_PLATFORM`.

#### `POST /auth/signup-lister`
```ts
interface Req { role: 'lister'; username: string; email: string; org_name: string; website_url?: string; }
interface Res { user_id: string; jwt: string; }
```
Side effects: `users` + `lister_profiles` insert. Issues custom JWT.
Errors: `409 USERNAME_TAKEN`, `409 EMAIL_TAKEN`.

#### `GET /auth/oauth-callback/:platform`
Handle redirect from TikTok/IG. Exchange code → tokens → user_info → find-or-create user.
```ts
interface Query { code: string; state: string; }
interface Res { jwt: string; user_id: string; is_new: boolean; }
```
Side effects: `users`+`creator_profiles`+`social_links` upsert; enqueues initial metric fetch; emits event; deletes `oauth_states` row.
Errors: `400 INVALID_STATE`, `401 OAUTH_FAILED`, `409 ACCOUNT_LINKED_TO_OTHER_USER`.

#### `POST /auth/signout`
```ts
interface Req { session_id: string; }
interface Res { ok: true; }
```
Since deleting a user does NOT invalidate JWTs (per Supabase security note), we keep a `session_id` claim and a server-side `revoked_sessions` set (redis/KV or `app_private.revoked_sessions` table) checked by an edge middleware.

### 5.2 Social Accounts & Metrics

#### `POST /social/link-start`
Start linking an additional platform to an existing user.
```ts
interface Req { platform: 'tiktok' | 'instagram'; }
interface Res { auth_url: string; state: string; }
```
Auth: creator.

#### `POST /social/unlink`
```ts
interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
interface Res { ok: true; }
```
Auth: creator (owner). Errors: `409 LAST_LINK` if this is their only linked platform.

#### `POST /metrics/refresh`
Manually trigger metric refresh. Rate-limited.
```ts
interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
interface Res { snapshot_id: string; status: 'queued' | 'already_fresh'; }
```
Side effects: writes `metric_snapshots` (status=refreshing) and kicks background task. Idempotency: second call within 6 h returns `already_fresh`.
Errors: `429 RATE_LIMIT` (includes `retry_after`), `401 REAUTH_REQUIRED`, `502 PROVIDER_ERROR`.

#### Internal cron: `metrics-refresh-all`
Daily at 03:00 UTC. Picks links with `fetched_at < now() - 24h` in batches of 100, QPS-capped.

### 5.3 Listings — Browse (Creator)

#### `GET /rest/v1/listings?status=eq.active...` (PostgREST direct)
Feed query with filters. Client passes creator's denormalized metrics as query params for eligibility pre-filter, but the **authoritative** eligibility check runs in `/listings/:id/eligibility` before apply.

#### `GET /listings/:id` (Edge Function)
```ts
interface Res {
  listing: Listing; // active current_version joined
  conditions: ListingCondition[];
  sample_videos: SampleVideo[];
  eligibility: {
    eligible: boolean;
    failed_conditions: Array<{ metric, required, actual }>;
    has_active_application: boolean;
  };
}
```
Auth: creator or lister (lister sees their own).

### 5.4 Listings — Manage (Lister)

#### `POST /listings` — create
```ts
interface Req {
  title: string; description?: string;
  price_cents: number; currency?: string;
  max_submissions?: number | null;
  end_date?: string | null;
  pre_conditions: ListingConditionInput[];
  post_conditions: ListingConditionInput[];
  sample_videos: { platform: Platform; url: string; caption?: string; }[];
  publish: boolean; // false => draft
}
interface Res { listing_id: string; version_id: string; }
```
Side effects: `listings` + `listing_versions` + `listing_conditions` + `sample_videos` in a txn.

#### `PATCH /listings/:id` — update
```ts
interface Req { /* any subset of create */ }
interface Res { listing_id: string; version_id: string; cascaded_applications: number; }
```
Side effects: if versioned fields change, trigger cancels pending applications and sends notifications. Returns count of cascaded apps so UI can confirm.

#### `POST /listings/:id/status`
```ts
interface Req { action: 'publish' | 'pause' | 'resume' | 'close' | 'archive'; }
interface Res { ok: true; new_status: ListingStatus; }
```
Auth: lister (owner). Server validates state transition.

### 5.5 Applications

#### `POST /applications`
```ts
interface Req { listing_id: string; cover_note?: string; }
interface Res { application_id: string; listing_version_id: string; }
```
Idempotency: server checks partial-unique index; on collision returns existing `application_id`.
Side effects: re-evaluates eligibility on server; creates row; notifies lister (`application_received`).
Errors: `403 INELIGIBLE`, `409 ALREADY_APPLIED`, `409 LISTING_NOT_ACTIVE`.

#### `POST /applications/:id/withdraw` — creator
#### `POST /applications/:id/approve` — lister
Includes re-check of eligibility (may return `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`).
```ts
interface Req { override_ineligible?: boolean; decision_note?: string; }
```
#### `POST /applications/:id/reject` — lister
```ts
interface Req { decision_note?: string; }
```

### 5.6 Submissions

#### `POST /applications/:id/submissions`
```ts
interface Req { videos: { platform: Platform; url: string }[]; cover_note?: string; }
interface Res { submission_id: string; }
```
Side effects: validates each URL via oEmbed (422 if unreachable); creates submission + submission_videos; notifies lister (`submission_received`).
Errors: `403 APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED`, `409 SUBMISSION_EXISTS`, `422 INVALID_VIDEO_URL`.

#### `POST /submissions/:id/approve` — lister
#### `POST /submissions/:id/reject` — lister
#### `POST /submissions/:id/request-revision` — lister (only up to 2 times)
```ts
interface Req { decision_note?: string; }
```
#### `POST /submissions/:id/resubmit` — creator (when status=revision_requested)
```ts
interface Req { videos: { platform: Platform; url: string }[]; cover_note?: string; }
```

### 5.7 Notifications

#### `GET /rest/v1/notifications` — PostgREST direct read (RLS-scoped)
#### `POST /notifications/mark-read`
```ts
interface Req { ids: string[] | 'all'; }
interface Res { updated: number; }
```
#### **Realtime** — subscribe to `notifications` table filtered by `user_id=eq.<me>`. Client uses `supabase.channel('notif').on('postgres_changes', ...)`.
#### `POST /push/register`
```ts
interface Req { expo_token: string; platform: 'ios' | 'android'; }
interface Res { ok: true; }
```

### 5.8 Realtime Channels (summary)

| Channel | Who | Events |
|---|---|---|
| `notifications:user_id=<me>` | both | INSERT |
| `applications:listing_id=<id>` | lister | INSERT, UPDATE |
| `submissions:listing_id=<id>` | lister | INSERT, UPDATE |
| `applications:creator_id=<me>` | creator | UPDATE (approval/rejection) |
| `submissions:creator_id=<me>` | creator | UPDATE |

### 5.9 Webhooks

- **TikTok**: no webhook support for Display API metrics. Must poll.
- **Instagram**: webhooks exist for mentions/comments but not for follower_count changes. Skip in MVP.

---

## 6. Eligibility Evaluation Engine

### 6.1 Condition Schema (already in §4.7)

A pre-condition is a row: `(metric, platform, operator, numeric_threshold | text_threshold | bool_threshold)`.

Catalog:
| metric | platform | operator | threshold type |
|---|---|---|---|
| `min_followers` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
| `min_avg_views_last_n` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
| `min_total_likes` | tiktok | gte | numeric |
| `min_videos_posted` | tiktok \| instagram | gte | numeric |
| `verified_only` | tiktok \| instagram | bool | boolean |

### 6.2 Evaluation Function Signature

```ts
type Metric = 'min_followers' | 'min_avg_views_last_n' | 'min_total_likes' | 'min_videos_posted' | 'verified_only';
interface Condition { metric: Metric; platform: 'tiktok' | 'instagram'; operator: 'gte' | 'bool'; threshold: number | boolean; }
interface CreatorMetrics {
  tiktok: { follower_count?: number; avg_views_last_10?: number; total_likes?: number; video_count?: number; is_verified?: boolean; fetched_at?: string } | null;
  instagram: { follower_count?: number; avg_views_last_10?: number; media_count?: number; fetched_at?: string } | null;
}
interface EligibilityResult { eligible: boolean; failed: Array<{condition: Condition; actual: number | boolean | null}>; stale_platforms: string[]; }

function evaluate(conditions: Condition[], metrics: CreatorMetrics): EligibilityResult { ... }
```

### 6.3 Where It Runs

- **Client-side (fast UI):** on app resume we fetch `creator_profiles` row once into a context. Feed query returns listings with their `pre_conditions` inlined; client filters locally. Gives instant "eligible only" toggle without a round-trip per listing.
- **Server-side (authoritative):** `POST /applications` runs the exact same TypeScript function on Deno (Edge Function), reading the same denormalized metrics from `creator_profiles`. No trust in client filter.
- **At approve-time (re-check):** `POST /applications/:id/approve` re-runs evaluation using current metrics, NOT the metrics at apply-time. (Decision #12.)

### 6.4 Scaling Feed to 1000+ Listings

- Denormalize `min_followers_tiktok` and `min_followers_instagram` onto `listings` as generated columns (MAX across pre-conditions per platform). Add indexes.
- Feed query:
  ```sql
  select * from public.listings
  where status = 'active'
    and (min_followers_tiktok is null or min_followers_tiktok <= $creator_tt_followers)
    and (min_followers_ig is null or min_followers_ig <= $creator_ig_followers)
  order by created_at desc
  limit 50;
  ```
- More complex conditions (avg views, video count) evaluated client-side after this first-pass server filter trims to hundreds.
- **No need for full-text search or ElasticSearch in MVP.** Postgres btree indexes suffice.

---

## 7. Critical Workflow Sequence Diagrams

### 7.1 Creator Signup + Social Link + Initial Fetch

```
App               EdgeFn(signup)          TikTok OAuth        EdgeFn(callback)      DB
 │─POST /auth/signup-creator──>│                                                       │
 │                              │─insert oauth_states────────────────────────────────> │
 │<─{auth_url, state}───────────│                                                       │
 │──open auth_url──────────────────────────────> │                                      │
 │                                              │─user approves──>│                     │
 │<─redirect ?code&state─────────────────────────────────────────│                     │
 │─GET /auth/oauth-callback/tiktok?code&state──────────────────>│                      │
 │                                                              │─exchange code────>TT│
 │                                                              │<─access_token──────│
 │                                                              │─GET user/info─────>TT│
 │                                                              │<─follower_count,..──│
 │                                                              │─upsert user + sl──>│
 │                                                              │─insert metric snap─>│
 │                                                              │─enqueue video/list bg│
 │<─{jwt, user_id}───────────────────────────────────────────────│                     │
 │                                                                                      │
 (background, ~2s later)
 │                                              Worker          │─POST video/list───>TT│
 │                                                              │<─videos───────────────│
 │                                                              │─update snapshot────>│
 │                                                              │─(trigger)denorm────>│
Realtime push: metrics ready
```

### 7.2 Creator Apply (with Eligibility Check)

```
App                   Client cache              EdgeFn(applications)        DB
 │─tap Apply─────────────>│                                                    │
 │ eligibility eval local │                                                    │
 │─POST /applications────────────────────────────>│                            │
 │                                                │─SELECT listing+version+conds│
 │                                                │─SELECT creator_profiles────│
 │                                                │─evaluate() server-side─────│
 │                                                │  eligible?                  │
 │                                                │─INSERT applications────────>│
 │                                                │─INSERT notification(lister)>│
 │<─{application_id}──────────────────────────────│                             │
 │                                                                              │
Realtime: lister receives INSERT on applications channel
```

### 7.3 Lister Edit Listing → Cascade

```
Lister app       EdgeFn(listings update)       DB (trigger)              Affected creators
 │─PATCH /listings/:id price────────>│                                     │
 │                                    │─UPDATE listings price_cents────────>│
 │                                    │                   trg_bump_version │
 │                                    │                   ├─INSERT listing_versions
 │                                    │                   ├─UPDATE applications SET status=cancelled_listing_edit
 │                                    │                   └─INSERT notifications
 │<─{version_id, cascaded=12}─────────│                                     │
                                                                   Realtime push to each creator
```

### 7.4 Creator Submit → Lister Review

```
Creator app       EdgeFn(submissions)          oEmbed (TikTok/IG)     DB              Lister app
 │─POST /applications/:id/submissions────>│                                             │
 │                                         │─validate URLs via oEmbed─>│                │
 │                                         │<─200/404─────────────────│                │
 │                                         │─INSERT submissions + videos──────>│        │
 │                                         │─INSERT notification(lister)──────>│        │
 │<─{submission_id}────────────────────────│                                  │ Realtime├─>lister UI updates
 │                                                                             │         │
 │                                            Lister reviews, approves         │         │
 │                                         <─POST /submissions/:id/approve─────────────│
 │                                         │─UPDATE submissions status=approved>│        │
 │                                         │         (trigger auto_close_on_max)│        │
 │                                         │─INSERT notification(creator)──────>│        │
 │ Realtime update ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────│        │
```

### 7.5 Metrics Refresh (Rate Limited + Failure)

```
Creator app    EdgeFn(metrics/refresh)    Rate limiter (edge KV)    Platform API     DB
 │─POST /metrics/refresh sl_id─>│                                                      │
 │                                │─check last_refresh_at > 6h?──>│                    │
 │                                │<─allow/deny──────────────────│                    │
 │  (denied)                                                                            │
 │<─429 {retry_after}─────────────│                                                      │
 │  (allowed)                                                                            │
 │                                │─INSERT snapshot status=refreshing─────────────>DB   │
 │                                │─GET /v2/user/info/────────────>│                    │
 │                                │<─200 or 401 or 5xx─────────────│                    │
 │  (success)                                                                            │
 │                                │─UPDATE snapshot status=fresh + data──────────>DB    │
 │                                │  (trigger denorm → creator_profiles)                │
 │<─{snapshot_id, status=fresh}──│                                                      │
 │  (401 → token expired)                                                                │
 │                                │─attempt refresh_token─────────>│                    │
 │                                │<─new access_token or fail──────│                    │
 │                                │  if fail: UPDATE snapshot failed, sl.fail_count++   │
 │                                │  if fail_count>=3: sl.status=failed_fetch           │
 │                                │  INSERT notification metrics_refresh_failed         │
 │<─{error: REAUTH_REQUIRED}──────│                                                      │
```

---

## 8. MVP Scope Cuts

Sorted by "most likely to ship day one" impact.

1. **No payment system.** `price` is a display string. Off-platform handshake. Cut: Stripe Connect, KYC, escrow, disputes-with-money. Defer to v2.

2. **OAuth required even though "no auth" is the PRD wish.** Keeping this full-scope is non-negotiable per §3e. But we DO cut email/password signup entirely — OAuth is the ONLY way in for creators. (Listers use email/username.)

3. **Instagram metrics gated behind Business/Creator account requirement.** If the creator has a personal IG account, we show: "Instagram metrics require a free conversion to Creator account. Continue with TikTok only?" — do NOT build a workaround. Defer any personal-IG support.

4. **No post-submission performance tracking in v1.** We store the video URL and embed it. We do NOT track view_count growth, ROI, etc. Cut this; sell it in v2 as "Marketify Analytics."

5. **No lister dashboard analytics.** Just list views of their campaigns + applications + submissions. No charts, no funnel. Cut.

6. **No admin moderation console.** Reports go into a table, admin reads it via Supabase Studio. No in-app admin tooling.

7. **No revision-request flow in v1.** Cut `revision_requested` state; lister either approves or rejects. Reduces submission state machine from 5 states to 3. (Easy to add back; the submission table already has the column.)
   - *Tradeoff: worse creator experience when close-but-not-quite. Watch and re-add quickly.*

8. **No push notifications in v1.** In-app inbox only + Realtime. Expo Push adds provisioning friction (APNs cert, FCM key). Re-add in v1.1 once core loop works.

9. **Application/submission expiration timers: DO NOT cut.** Cheap to ship, keeps the system clean.

10. **Listing versioning + cascade-cancel: DO NOT cut.** This is the single trickiest business rule and is core to trust. Ship it.

11. **Metrics auto-refresh cron: DO NOT cut.** Required to keep eligibility honest. Ship it.

12. **Eligibility pre-filter client-side + server enforcement: DO NOT cut.** Core UX.

13. **Sample videos: simplify.** Store URL only; don't ship upload-to-Supabase-Storage. Use external URLs (TikTok/IG). Cut Storage integration for samples.

14. **Lister can "close" manually; no auto-close-on-max_submissions in v1.** Cut the trigger path. Add back once we see real usage patterns. (Tradeoff: listers must remember to close.)

15. **No creator blocklist, no lister blocklist.** Report-only.

16. **No in-app chat.** Out of scope.

---

## Appendix A: Open Questions for Product

These decisions were made unilaterally above and merit product review before build:

1. **OAuth mandatory for creators** (§3e, §8.2) — overrides PRD "no auth."
2. **No real money in MVP** (§1.18) — confirm with business.
3. **Post-condition changes DO cancel pending applications** (§1.10) — stricter than PRD literal text.
4. **14d application / 7d submission expiration** (§1.17) — numbers are my guess.
5. **Revision flow cut from v1** (§8.7) — user-facing impact.
6. **One role per account** (§1.3) — forces double-signup for creator-listers.
7. **Submission re-use across listings allowed** (§1.26) — flagged but not blocked.

---

**End of document.** Ready for review. When cuts are finalized, I can generate the first migration file via `supabase migration new marketify_init` and deploy skeleton edge functions.
